en: Shinan (Photinia leaf) — taste pungent, neutral. Nourishes kidney qi, treats internal damage and declining yin, benefits the sinews, bones, skin and hair. The fruit kills gu-poison, breaks up masses and accumulations, and expels wind-bi. Also called Guimu ("ghost eye"). Grows in mountain valleys.

Croton seed (Ba Dou) — taste pungent, warm, toxic. Indicated for cold-damage and warm malaria with chills and fever; breaks up concretions and hardened accumulations; treats retained fluids and phlegm nodules, and distension of the abdomen with fluid swelling. Purges the five viscera and six bowels, opens obstructions, and promotes passage through the water and food pathways; removes necrotic flesh, and eliminates ghost-toxin, gu-poison and evil influences. Kills insects and fish. Also called Bajiao. Grows in river valleys.

Sichuan pepper (Shu Jiao) — taste pungent, warm. Indicated for evil qi with cough and counterflow; warms the middle; expels numbness and dead flesh of the joints and skin, and cold-damp bi pain; directs qi downward. Taken long-term, it keeps the hair from graying, lightens the body, and prolongs life. Grows in river valleys.

Mangcao — taste pungent, warm. Indicated for wind-head disorders, carbuncles and swellings, breast abscess, and hernia-like masses; dispels bound qi, scabies and itching, and deep-rooted sores. Kills insects and fish. Grows in mountain valleys.

Yu kernel (bush-cherry pit) — taste sour, neutral. Indicated for abdominal water swelling, and swelling of the face, eyes, and limbs; promotes urination. The root treats swollen gums and dental caries, and strengthens the teeth. Rhamnus (Shu Li, buckthorn) treats chills-and-fever with scrofula sores. Also called Jueli. Grows in river valleys.
Luan flower (Koelreuteria/goldenrain-tree flower) — taste bitter, cold. Indicated for eye pain with tearing and damage to the canthus; reduces eye swelling. Grows in river valleys.

Manjiao — taste bitter, warm. Indicated for wind-cold-damp bi and pain in the joints throughout the body; dispels counterflow qi in the limbs, and knee pain. Also called Shijiao ("hog pepper"). Grows in river valleys.

Leiwan (Omphalia) — taste bitter, cold. Governs killing the three worms, and expelling toxic qi and heat from the stomach; beneficial for men, not beneficial for women. Made into a rubbing ointment, it treats the hundred diseases of children. Grows in mountain valleys.
Soushu (Deutzia) — taste pungent, cold. Indicated for heat in the skin and body; dispels evil qi; stops enuresis. May be used to make a bathing decoction. Grows in river valleys.

Yaoshi root (the root of Beimu, fritillary bulb) — taste pungent, warm. Indicated for evil qi and various aching bi conditions; mends severed injuries; supplements the bone marrow. Also called Lianmu. (This is the root of Beimu.) Grows in mountain valleys.

Gleditsia pod (Zaojia, Chinese honey-locust pod) — taste pungent, salty, warm. Indicated for wind-bi and numb dead flesh, evil qi, and wind-head with tearing eyes. Opens the nine orifices; kills malevolent spirits. Grows in river valleys.

Lianshi (Melia/chinaberry fruit) — taste bitter, cold. Indicated for warm-disease and cold-damage with high fever, vexation and mania; kills the three worms; treats scabies and sores; promotes urination. (This is the fruit of the Melia tree.) Grows in mountain valleys.

Willow catkin (Liu Hua) — taste bitter, cold. Indicated for wind edema, jaundice, and facial heat with darkening. The leaves treat scabby sores in horses. The fruit treats ruptured carbuncles, expelling pus and blood. Also called Liuxu ("willow floss"). Grows in the river marshes of Langye.

Paulownia leaf (Tong Ye) — taste bitter, cold. Indicated for malignant erosive sores of the genitals. The bark treats the five kinds of hemorrhoids and kills the three worms. The flower, applied to sores on pigs and fed to them, makes them grow three times fatter. Grows in mountain valleys.

Processed human hair (Fabi) — taste bitter, warm. Indicated for the five stranguries with impassable blockage; promotes urination; treats infantile epilepsy and adult tetany. It also has the marvelous property of transforming back into its original substance.

Dragon bone (Longgu, fossilized bone) — taste sweet, neutral. Indicated for ghost-attachment of the heart and abdomen, malevolent spirits and old demons; cough with counterflow; diarrhea with pus and blood; women's flooding and spotting; concretions and hardened masses; infantile fright-epilepsy from heat qi. The teeth treat fright-epilepsy, mania and running madness in children and adults, bound qi below the heart with inability to breathe, and various convulsions; they kill malevolent spirits. Taken long-term, it lightens the body, opens spirit-brightness, and prolongs life. Grows in mountain valleys.

Bovine bezoar (Niuhuang, ox gallstone) — taste bitter, neutral. Indicated for fright-epilepsy with chills and fever, and severe heat with mania and tetany; dispels evil and expels ghosts. The base of the ox horn treats blocked blood, static blood with pain, and women's vaginal discharge with bleeding. The marrow supplements the middle and fills the bone marrow; taken long-term, it prolongs life. The gallbladder can be made into pills. Grows in level marshes.

Musk (Shexiang) — taste pungent, warm. Governs warding off malign qi, and killing ghosts and malevolent spirits; treats warm malaria, gu-poison, epilepsy and tetany; removes the three worms. Taken long-term, it dispels evil influences and prevents nightmares and sleep paralysis. Grows in river valleys.

Bear fat (Xiong Zhi) — taste sweet, slightly cold. Indicated for wind-bi with numbness and sinew spasm; accumulations and masses in the five viscera and abdomen; chills and fever with emaciation; scalp sores and various forms of baldness; facial spots and blemishes. Taken long-term, it strengthens the will, suppresses hunger, and lightens the body. Grows in mountain valleys.

White glue (deer-antler glue, Baijiao) — taste sweet, neutral. Indicated for internal injury and exhaustion, lower-back pain with emaciation; supplements the middle and boosts qi; women's amenorrhea and infertility; stops pain and calms the fetus. Taken long-term, it lightens the body and prolongs life. Also called Deer Antler Glue (Lujiaojiao).

Donkey-hide glue (Ejiao) — taste sweet, neutral. Indicated for internal collapse of the heart and abdomen, extreme exhaustion with chills resembling malaria, lower-back and abdominal pain, aching of the four limbs, and women's vaginal bleeding; calms the fetus. Taken long-term, it lightens the body and boosts qi. Also called Chuanzhijiao.

Red rooster (Dan Xiong Ji) — taste sweet, slightly warm. Indicated for women's flooding and spotting, and red or white vaginal discharge; supplements deficiency, warms the middle, stops bleeding, opens the spirit, kills toxin, and wards off inauspicious influences. The head kills ghosts. The fat treats deafness. The intestines treat enuresis. The yellow lining of the gizzard treats diarrhea. The white droppings treat wasting-thirst and cold-damage with chills and fever. The wing feathers treat blocked menstruation. The egg removes heat and fire-sores, epilepsy and tetany, and can be made into an amber-like marvelous substance. White grubs found on chickens fatten pigs. Grows in level marshes.

Wild goose fat (Yan Fang) — taste sweet, neutral, non-toxic. Indicated for wind contracture and spasm, hemiplegia, and impeded qi flow. Taken long-term, it boosts qi, suppresses hunger, lightens the body, and resists aging. Also called Wufang. Grows in ponds and marshes.

Honey (Shimi) — taste sweet, neutral. Indicated for evil qi in the heart and abdomen, and various fright-epilepsy and tetany conditions; calms deficiencies of the five viscera; boosts qi, supplements the middle, stops pain, resolves toxin, treats the multitude of diseases, and harmonizes the hundred medicines. Taken long-term, it strengthens the will, lightens the body, suppresses hunger, and prevents aging. Grows in mountain valleys.

Beeswax (Mila) — taste sweet, slightly warm. Indicated for diarrhea with pus and blood; supplements the middle, mends severed injuries and metal wounds, boosts qi, suppresses hunger, and resists aging. Grows in mountain valleys.

Bee larvae (Fengzi) — taste sweet, neutral. Indicated for wind-head disorders; dispels gu-poison; supplements deficiency and emaciation, and internal injury. Taken long-term, it gives one a lustrous glow and good complexion, and prevents aging. The larvae of large hornets treat distension and pain of the heart and abdomen, lightening the body and boosting qi. The larvae of ground wasps treat carbuncles and swellings. Also called Feiling. Grows in mountain valleys.
